data.content:
[plan] Implementation plan for exhaustive dispatch forensics

## Approach

Add a new self-contained `dispatch-forensics` module that captures the
required snapshots, persists them as standalone JSON files, emits one
greppable lease-audit event per snapshot, and runs a classifier on
post-turn snapshots to surface inconsistencies via a red banner. Wire
the capture function into existing boundaries — additive only, no
behavior change to dispatch flow itself.

## File-by-file

### New files

1. `src/lib/dispatch-forensics-types.ts` (~80 LOC)
   - `DispatchForensicBoundary` union type (10 boundary names)
   - `BeatSnapshot` interface (full beat JSON + lease list + metadata)
   - `ForensicCategory` union (`concurrent_claim_detected`,
     `kno_half_transition_suspected`,
     `our_agent_double_claim_suspected`,
     `lease_terminated_unexpectedly`, `unknown_state_change`)
   - `CaptureContext` interface (minimal subset of TakeLoopContext we
     need, decoupled to keep the module testable)

2. `src/lib/dispatch-forensics-storage.ts` (~120 LOC)
   - `DISPATCH_FORENSICS_SLUG = "_dispatch_forensics"`
   - `snapshotPath(date, sessionId, boundary, beatId, ts)` — pure path
   - `writeSnapshotFile(snapshot)` — persists to
     `.foolery-logs/_dispatch_forensics/<date>/<sessionId>/<ts>-<boundary>-<beatId>.json`
   - Uses the same logRoot resolution as lease-audit (so dev/installed
     paths Just Work)

3. `src/lib/dispatch-forensics-classify.ts` (~200 LOC)
   - `classifyTurnFailure(pre, post): ForensicClassification`
     - returns category + reasoning + "missing-from-pre" lease info
       (other agent's identity if a different lease appears bound)
   - `emitForensicBanner(category, info)` — uses
     `dispatch-pool-resolver.ts`'s ANSI red banner pattern
     (DO NOT duplicate the red-banner code; export a helper from
     dispatch-pool-resolver and call it)
   - Marker phrase: `FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC` (greppable, distinct
     from `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE`)

4. `src/lib/dispatch-forensics.ts` (~180 LOC)
   - `captureBeatSnapshot(boundary, ctx)`:
     - calls `showKnot(beatId, repoPath)` and
       `listLeases(repoPath, true)`
     - filters leases to those bound to this beat (or referenced in its
       active step_history)
     - assembles BeatSnapshot
     - writes the JSON file via storage helper
     - emits a lease-audit event with `event = beat_snapshot_<boundary>`
       and `data = { snapshotPath, ... }`
     - is fire-and-forget (returns void; never throws into the take
       loop)
   - `runPostTurnForensics(pre, post, ctx)`:
     - runs classifier; if non-null, emits banner + audit event +
       pushes a stderr banner event into the session UI buffer

5. `src/lib/__tests__/dispatch-forensics.test.ts` (~250 LOC)
   - hermetic; injects in-memory beat/lease state via a thin stub of
     showKnot/listLeases so no real fs/exec touches are made
   - cases:
     - capture happy path (snapshot persisted, audit event emitted,
       classifier returns null)
     - concurrent_claim_detected (a different lease appears in
       step_history between pre and post)
     - kno_half_transition_suspected (action step appears, our lease
       is unchanged, no other lease bound)
     - our_agent_double_claim_suspected (two action steps with our
       same lease)
     - lease_terminated_unexpectedly (our lease moves to
       lease_terminated without us calling terminate)
     - banner format includes the FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC marker
       and the category name

6. `src/lib/__manual_tests__/dispatch-forensics.manual.test.ts`
   (~100 LOC, in __manual_tests__ per Hermetic Test Policy)
   - Stands up a temporary knots repo, runs through a real claim+next
     cycle, asserts snapshots are written to the right paths with
     the right boundary names

### Modified files

7. `src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts`
   - Export the existing red-banner builder as a public helper
     (`buildAnsiRedBanner(plainBody)`) so dispatch-forensics-classify
     can reuse it without duplicating ANSI codes

8. `src/lib/knots-lease-runtime.ts`
   - In `ensureKnotsLease`: snapshot at `pre_lease` (right before
     createLease) and `post_lease` (right after success).
   - Snapshot calls are awaited NOT to block, but if logRoot resolution
     is async, fire as void

9. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-prompt.ts`
   - Snapshot at `pre_prompt_build` right before
     `getBackend().buildTakePrompt`

10. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-agent.ts`
    - Snapshot at `pre_prompt_send` after binding check, before the
      child receives the prompt
    - Snapshot at `post_prompt_ack` when transport reports
      promptDeliverySucceeded

11. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child-helpers.ts` or
    `terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts`
    - Snapshot at `post_turn_success` when child closes with code 0
    - Snapshot at `post_turn_failure` when child closes with non-zero
    - Run `runPostTurnForensics` on failure

12. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-follow-up.ts`
    - Snapshot at `pre_followup` right before
      `take_loop_follow_up_sent`

13. `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-loop.ts`
    - Periodic snapshot timer (every 30s during turn) — snapshot kind
      `periodic`
    - Snapshot at `post_rollback` when the take-loop detects the beat
      moved back to a queue state mid-turn

## Concurrency / performance

- Snapshot writes are fully async. Each snapshot fires `void` and
  doesn't block the take loop.
- Snapshot file size: a single beat JSON is typically <50 KB; lease
  list is <10 KB. With ~10 snapshots per turn that's <600 KB per
  turn — acceptable.
- Periodic snapshot interval: 30s default (configurable via const).
  Cancelled when turn ends.

## Test strategy

- Hermetic unit tests (per Hermetic Test Policy): inject in-memory
  showKnot/listLeases; no real fs, no real exec. Use a thin storage
  stub that captures writes in memory.
- Manual integration test (in `__manual_tests__/`, excluded from CI):
  exercises against a real knots repo to verify file paths/names.

## Risks

- `showKnot`/`listLeases` are exec calls (kno CLI). If they hang,
  snapshot capture must NOT block the take loop. Use Promise.race
  with a short timeout (e.g. 5s) and log a warning if it times out.
- Classifier false positives: must err on the side of "I don't know,
  unknown_state_change" rather than confidently misclassifying.
- File volume: 10 snapshots/turn × many turns/day could grow. Out
  of scope for this knot — separate cleanup knot if needed.

## Out of scope (explicit)

- Behavior fixes (back-off-on-concurrent-claim, no-double-claim,
  rollback-and-retry) — separate knots that DEPEND on the answers
  this knot's logging produces.
- The take-loop dead-lease check (foolery-2dd7's job).

## Acceptance — full match to knot description

- `captureBeatSnapshot(boundary, ctx)` API exists in
  `dispatch-forensics.ts` and is called at all 10 boundaries.
- Snapshots stored under
  `.foolery-logs/_dispatch_forensics/<date>/<sessionId>/`.
- Lease audit JSONL has new event names with snapshotPath references.
- `FOOLERY DISPATCH FORENSIC` marker phrase used in banners.
- Hermetic unit tests cover all 5 classifier categories.
- Manual integration test in `__manual_tests__/`.
- Lint/typecheck/build/test green.
